description: Design an API gateway and Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) layer for Contoso's multi-channel commerce platform. The gateway fronts five downstream microservices (catalog, cart, checkout, identity, notifications) and provides client-specific aggregation for web SPA, mobile native, and partner B2B API consumers.
constraints: Gateway latency overhead must be < 15ms p99 for pass-through routes
Rate limiting per API key with configurable burst allowance per partner tier
Must support gradual traffic shifting for canary deployments of downstream services
requiredCapabilities: Azure API Management with self-hosted gateway option for edge locations
OAuth 2.0 token exchange (external IdP token → internal service token)
Response caching at gateway for catalog reads (TTL configurable per product category)
assumptions: Mobile and web clients authenticate via Entra External ID (B2C replacement)
Partner B2B clients use client_credentials flow with pre-registered app registrations
inlineRequirements: Document circuit-breaker behavior: what the client sees when a downstream service is unhealthy

documents.name: api-inventory.txt
documents.content: Fictional downstream APIs: Catalog (read-heavy, cacheable), Cart (session-scoped, low latency), Checkout (transactional), Identity (auth flows), Notifications (async, fire-and-forget).

topologyHints: BFF pattern: separate aggregation layers for web (SSR-friendly) and mobile (minimal payload)
Prefer APIM policies over custom code for cross-cutting concerns
securityBaselineHints: Validate JWT at the gateway; do not forward raw external tokens to downstream services
IP allow-listing for partner B2B tier; geo-blocking for sanctioned regions
